clue: Mile without hard shoulder... start to get angry
explanation: Mile (M) without hard (H) in SHOULDER gives SMOULDER, meaning "start to get angry".
hints: "Mile" and "hard" can each be represented by a single letter.
The definition is "start to get angry", though we're talking a little figuratively.
What happens if we take "shoulder", take out "hard", and add "mile" in its place?
